name: Trundle

basic_attack.calculation_notes: Basic-attack timing values are sourced from From Online stats payload.

passive.name: King's Tribute
passive.description_source: Innate: Enemies that die near  Trundle cause him to  heal based on their maximum health.

effects.description: Innate: Whenever a nearby enemy dies, Trundle heals himself for 1.8% : 5.94% (based on level) of their maximum health.

passive.description: Innate: Whenever a nearby enemy dies, Trundle heals himself for 1.8% : 5.94% (based on level) of their maximum health.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): King's Tribute does not trigger when an enemy structure is destroyed.

basic_ability_1.name: Chomp
basic_ability_1.description_source: Active:  Trundle's next  basic attack within a few seconds will deal bonus physical damage and briefly  slow the target.

effects.context_notes: Active: Trundle empowers his next basic attack within 7 seconds to have an uncancellable windup, gain 25 bonus range, deal bonus physical damage and slow the target by 75% for 0.1 seconds.
After using the empowered attack, Trundle gains bonus attack damage for 5 seconds and reduces the target's bonus attack damage by half that amount for the same duration.
After using the empowered attack, Trundle gains bonus attack damage for 5 seconds and reduces the target's bonus attack damage by half that amount for the same duration.

effects.description: Chomp resets Trundle's basic attack timer.

basic_ability_1.description: Active: Trundle empowers his next basic attack within 7 seconds to have an uncancellable windup, gain 25 bonus range, deal bonus physical damage and slow the target by 75% for 0.1 seconds.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Chomp will still incur the attack damage increase for Trundle even if the attack is dodged, blocked, missed while he is blinded, or it is blocked by spell shield.
Manual review (2026-02-24): Chomp is cast-gated and attack-cadence-coupled (auto reset plus empowered strike), so damage/slow resolve on attack contact rather than instantly on cast.
Manual review (2026-02-24): Intended behavior keeps the self attack-damage gain even if the empowered swing fails to deal hit damage due to miss/parry/blind/spell-shield edge cases.

basic_ability_2.name: Frozen Domain
basic_ability_2.description_source: Active:  Trundle coats the target location in ice for a period. While he is within the area, he gains  bonus attack speed,  bonus movement speed, and increased  healing from all sources.

effects.context_notes: Active: Trundle coats the target location in ice for 8 seconds.
Active: Trundle coats the target location in ice for 8 seconds.

basic_ability_2.description: Active: Trundle coats the target location in ice for 8 seconds. While he is within the area, he gains bonus attack speed, bonus movement speed, and 25% increased healing from all sources.

basic_ability_3.name: Pillar of Ice
basic_ability_3.description_source: Active:  Trundle erects an ice pillar at the target location that remains for a few seconds, which  knocks back units hit. The pillar acts as terrain and  slows nearby enemies.

effects.context_notes: Active: Trundle erects a pillar of ice at the target location for 6 seconds, which knocks back units hit to 225 units from its center.

basic_ability_3.description: Active: Trundle erects a pillar of ice at the target location for 6 seconds, which knocks back units hit to 225 units from its center. The pillar acts as terrain and slows nearby enemies.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Pillar of Ice displaces allied units away from the area but does not render them airborne.

ultimate.name: Subjugate
ultimate.description_source: Active:  Trundle drains the life force of the target enemy champion, dealing magic damage based on their maximum health and  healing for the same amount. He also steals a portion of their  armor and  magic resist, and increases in  size while reducing their  size.

effects.context_notes: Active: Trundle drains the life force out of the target enemy champion, dealing magic damage and healing himself for the same amount.
Active: Trundle drains the life force out of the target enemy champion, dealing magic damage and healing himself for the same amount.
Half of the drain's total damage, healing, and resistances reduction is applied on-cast, while the other half is applied every second over the next 4 seconds.
Half of the drain's total damage, healing, and resistances reduction is applied on-cast, while the other half is applied every second over the next 4 seconds.
Half of the drain's total damage, healing, and resistances reduction is applied on-cast, while the other half is applied every second over the next 4 seconds.
Half of the drain's total damage, healing, and resistances reduction is applied on-cast, while the other half is applied every second over the next 4 seconds.

effects.description: The resistances reduction on the target will linger for 4 seconds after the drain has ended.

ultimate.description: Active: Trundle drains the life force out of the target enemy champion, dealing magic damage and healing himself for the same amount. He also reduces 40% of their current armor and magic resistance, while gaining the same amount reduced as bonus armor and bonus magic resistance, respectively, for himself. Additionally, he increases in size by 18% while reducing the target's size by 9.9%.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Subjugate will snapshot the values of the target's current maximum health, total armor, and total magic resistance as soon as it completes its cast.

sources.used_for: canonical champion baseline generation for stats, abilities, and execution metadata

sources.type: League of Legends Wiki (ability data template)
sources.used_for: manual behavior verification for Chomp attack-reset timing and hit/miss edge semantics

notes: Generated from From Online champion payload as part of champion corpus parity coverage.
Ability execution metadata is source-derived from castTime/speed/effectRadius when present.
This baseline should be manually refined for high-impact simulation nuances before runtime scripting expansion.

schema_notes.base_by_rank: Arrays indexed by ability rank (1-based in game, 0-based in data).
schema_notes.base_by_champion_level: Arrays indexed by champion level minus one.
schema_notes.formula: Formula objects use explicit op/terms/output_stat when reliable source math is available.
schema_notes.ratio_type: Common ratio types: total_ad, bonus_ad, ap, target_max_health, target_missing_health.
schema_notes.value_percent: Percent values are represented as whole percentages unless noted otherwise.
schema_notes.basic_attack: Basic attack timing and projectile defaults are source-derived with documented fallbacks.
schema_notes.basic_attack_raw_timing_stats: Raw timing stats preserve source payload fields for auditability.
